title: Synthesis and Characterization of Silver Nanoparticles using Sodium Borohydride as a Reducing Agent
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7iaf2","text":"Silver nanoparticles (AgNP) have a multitude of uses, which range from anti-microbial to anti-cancerous etc. They are sometimes preferred to other metal-based nanoparticles due to their attractive physicochemical properties.  This protocol is a standardized method of producing and characterising PVA coated AgNPs which range from 10 to 20 nm in diameter. These particles are suited to lab based in-vivo and in-vitro assays. The addition of PVA to AgNPs is to increase the stability of the nanoparticles. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7olg0","text":"While performing Step 5 ensure droplets are added into the vortex, avoiding contact with the walls of the beaker. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":17,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bpsi5","text":"Before starting the process,  set up an ice bath that contains a magnetic stir plate. This will be used throughout the synthesis process. warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bj5do","text":"PPE must be worn when handling sodium borohydride,  as it poses the following risks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dcacv","text":" H260: In contact with water releases flammable gases which may ignite spontaneously [Danger Substances and mixtures which in contact with water, emit flammable gases]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1da0g","text":"H301: Toxic if swallowed [Danger Acute toxicity, oral]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8hm2n","text":"H311: Toxic in contact with skin [Danger Acute toxicity, dermal]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d52e0","text":"H314: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage [Danger Skin corrosion/irritation]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2f09o","text":"H318: Causes serious eye damage [DangerSerious eye damage/eye irritation]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b412k","text":"H332: Harmful if inhaled [WarningAcute toxicity, inhalation]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"41hk3","text":"H360: May damage fertility or the unborn child [Danger Reproductive toxicity]","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 30 mL of 0.002 Molarity (M) Sodium Borohydride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #213462-25G to an Erlenmeyer flask.
Insert the Erlenmeyer flask containing 30 mL of sodium borohydride into the previously set up ice bath containing the stir plate and allow to cool for 00:20:00
Place a magnetic mixing rod into the Erlenmeyer flask and start the stir plate to initiate mixing.

In another Erlenmeyer flask, transfer 2 mL of the freshly prepared Silver Nitrite Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #209139-25G along with 2 mL of a 1% PVA solution. 1% Polyvinyl Alcohol in Millipore water (Simplicity 185, 18.2 MΩ.cm at 25°C resistivity)

At a rate of one drop per second, drip 2 mL of the AgNO3/PVA solution into the chilled Sodium Borohydride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #213462-25G solution, maintaining the cooling and mixing simultaneously.

When the AgNO3/PVA solution has been added to the Sodium Borohydride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #213462-25G solution, stop mixing immediately. Once the reaction has been stopped, note that a yellow solution should be formed. This indicates that PVA-AgNP formation has occurred.

Transfer 20 mL of the PVA-AgNP solution, along with the excess Sodium Borohydride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #213462-25G into an ultrafiltration tube of 3kDa. 3kDa Ultrafiltration tube used in this protocol was from Sartorius. This step may need to be repeated for volumes >20mL.
Transfer the tube into a centrifuge and run at 5000 rpm, Room temperature, 40 minutes

Post centrifugation, smaller NPs (>5μm in diameter) and excess water (including unwanted reactants) can be discarded from the ultrafiltration tube. The smaller AgNPs will be seen at the clustered at the bottom of the tube.
The PVA-AgNP concentrate will display a dark brown colour, this is then collected using a dropper.
The harvested AgNPs must then be stored at fridge temperature (Approx. 4°C) away from direct sunlight.
AgNP samples are prepared using a 1 in 10 dilution by adding 1 ml concentrated PVA-AgNP to 9 mL of millipore water
For primary characterisation, 3.5 mL of PVA-AgNP's are placed into a cuvette, a UV-Vis spectrum is ran from 300nm-600nm. Strong absorbance at 400nm indicates 10mm AgNP synthesis.

Using a DLS instrument (Malvern Instruments, UK) particle size range is measured by placing 3.5 mL PVA-AgNP soloution into a 12mm o.d. square polystyrene cuvette and ran as per manufactuer specification. DLS Measures hydrodynamic size, including both the PVA coating the AgNP's. Zetasizer software was used to calculate the size distribution.

The DLS instrument was again used to measure the Zeta Potential of the AgNPs, this was performed on the same sample specifications for the previous DLS sample go to step #14 . Zeta potential measures the potential for the particles to flocculate, with a high negative or positive charge causing them to repel one another.

STEM Characterization, 3 µL of PVA-AgNP aqueous solution is placed onto a carbon-coated copper plate and allowed to dry. Particles are then analyzed visually for dispersion and shape. Drying allows for the highest yield of particles on the grid.
Using the previously prepared calibration solutions (1-5 ug AgNP/mL) the concentration of the prepared AgNP solution was calculated using AAS (Varian SpectrAA 200).
creator: James F Curtin
